channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Fly-tipped armchair and fridge, 5th March
item.description:
Please remove an armchair and fridge fly-tipped outside of Trinity Court, Sydenham Park near corner of Sydenham Park Road yesterday.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sydenham Park, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Water leakedge, 5th March
item.description:
Hello, appoligise its not drain issue but its close to it and cant find better category, also some works was done at this point but unsure if what related. Its some water coming out from the ground, and driping from the cerb. Where is a water meter near by, so I suspect its could be damage to water supply line.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravensbourne Avenue, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 82 - 120 Howbury Lane bins on the pavment, 5th March
item.description:
Residents from 82 - 120 Howbury Lane are leaving their bins on the pavement which is obstructing the footpath. People are having to walk in the road. Please can this be looked into asap. Some of them are being left in the road to reserve a parking space.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Howbury Lane, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pallet on pavement os 100 - 102 Howbury Lane, 5th March
item.description:
There is a pallet on the pavement os 100 - 102 Howbury Lane which is obstructing the footpath. People are having to walk in the road to get by including parents with pushchairs so it is very dangerous. Please can this be looked into asap.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Howbury Lane, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Builders damaging grass verges, 5th March
item.description:
2 houses are having extensive work over months now at the end of Broxbourne Road near Chislehurst Road. Both have been parking on the green verges or using them to turn round. The verges have huge tyre marks on them and now one lot of builders have a lot of building materials on the grass verge. This appears not lit at night or has no barrier around it. When asking them not to park on the verges some have been abusive so I am unable to take photos without them seeing. Who is liable to make good our grass verges?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Broxbourne Road, Crofton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fridge Freezer and Rubbish Dumped, 5th March
item.description:
Fridge Freezer & bags rubbish dumped just outside to the left of the army cadet building

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 159, Bexley Road, Northumberland Heath, Erith, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pink writing on stairwell, 5th March
item.description:
Looks like someone had reported the bit on the steel post but not on the wall opposite. This has been here a few days now.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Knee Hill, Abbey Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large and deep pothole, 5th March
item.description:
There is a large and deep pothole inside the yellow markings box, as well other smaller ones. it has been there for long time, but has recently got a lot worse. car are driving around it as it is fairly deep, this could lead to an accident.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 88, Bexley High Street, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The grass cutting team that worked in The Avenue this morning did not cut the grass on the approach to Redroofs Close, 5th March
item.description:
The grass cutting team that worked in The Avenue this morning did not cut the grass on the approach to Redroofs Close
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Redroofs Close, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Shopping Trolly, 5th March
item.description:
Shopping Trolly Abandoned on grass area between two blocks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Riverpark Gardens, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox on public foot path , penn lane next to A2, 5th March
item.description:
Dead fox on walkway

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): East Rochester Way, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Dead animal in the carriageway or footway

item.title: Street sign knocked over, 5th March
item.description:
Been like it for a couple of days.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southborough Lane, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unattended uncut public grass area, 5th March
item.description:
Please note this grass area is public not private and should be cut regularly especially during summer. Please call us on (07976) 121234 to let us know you are aware of this. Many thanks and kind regards, Stephen Sparling.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Surrey Water Road, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tent in Princess Diana Garden on estate, 5th March
item.description:
Camping and associated hygiene issues.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Thornbury Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Reported blue bins not collected for past 2 weeks, 5th March
item.description:
Reported that blue bins not collected for past 2 weeks, but has been updated as fixed. Reporting again as issue not resolved and cannot wait for another week. This needs to be collected today!
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bassant Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Old suitcase dumped, 5th March
item.description:
Old suitcase dumped in Theodore Road today. Close to HGL Coop

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Theodore Road, Hither Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The waste from bins is all over the road in from of house numbers: 86, 94, 48,50,52., 5th March
item.description:
Waste has not been collected from some these bins outside these properties which are now overflowing on the public walk way and obstructing the public path. The food waste is attracting vermin. Some of the gardens also contain accumulated waste. Why are the council not collecting this waste? Please could you write to the occupiers or landlords for them to action this by re moving the waste. Or serve notices or carry out works in default and recharge the property owner? Thank you.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chestnut Rise, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 public litter bins, 5th March
item.description:
Resident has called to report the 2 public litter bins by the bus shelter needs to be emptied on a regular basis. Please can this be investigated.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blackbrook Lane, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Potholes - very deep, before Crouch Farm Cottages and also Woodview, 5th March
item.description:
The potholes along the length of Crockenhill Road have been there for months. In February there were traffic lights installed but that was not for the potholes to be repaired but for the water board. Please sort this very busy road out as it is a vital connection through Crockenhill.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crockenhill Road, St Mary Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The trees in Baynes Street Canalside Garden, 5th March
item.description:
The trees in the garden are encroaching on the southern aspect of Reachview Close and there seems to be some movement of the earth towards the garden which could be due to the over density and size of trees extracting all the moisture (even in recent months)
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Regent's Canal towpath, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
